title: Epitaph On The Lady Mary Villiers
content:
The Lady Mary Villiers lies
    Under this stone; with weeping eyes
    The parents that first gave her birth,
    And their sad friends, laid her in earth.
    If any of them, Reader, were
    Known unto thee, shed a tear;
    Or if thyself possess a gem
    As dear to thee, as this to them,
    Though a stranger to this place,
    Bewail in theirs thine own hard case:
    For thou perhaps at thy return
    May�st find thy Darling in an urn.
meaning:
Thomas Carew's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Epitaph On The Lady Mary Villiers"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
